title: Capture power intent in UPF or CPF
description: Declare power domains, supply nets, isolation, level shifters, and retention in a power-intent file the tools enforce.
body: Use when a design has more than one power state or voltage domain. Do not bury power intent in scattered RTL, since tools need a single declarative source to insert and check power structures. Actions: define the power domains and supply nets, declare isolation and retention strategies, and verify the tools insert matching cells. Validation signals include a power-intent file that the tools accept without errors and inserted cells matching the declared strategy. Risk boundary: a mismatch between intent and RTL silently breaks power management, so deny sign-off until the power-intent file checks clean.
